summary:
The statement that the image in indirect ophthalmoscopy is visually erect is false because real images produced by convex lenses—used during this procedure—are inherently inverted. This directly conflicts with basic geometrical optics principles applicable to such examinations.  

Note: Only include information from Agent1's response; do not add external knowledge or assumptions.

Only summarize what was said in the provided text above – no additions, interpretations, or explanations beyond those made by Agent1.

The claim that the image in indirect ophthalmoscopy is erect is false because convex lenses form inverted (real) images when objects lie beyond their focal point. According to geometric optics fundamentals, these resulting images must be inverted rather than erect.
